current_entries.en_definition: One of four seasons, traditionally the second, marked by the longest and typically hottest days of the year due to the inclination of the Earth and thermal lag. Typically regarded as spanning the months of June, July, and August in the Northern Hemisphere and the months of December, January and February in the Southern Hemisphere.

examples.english: During summer breaks, I ate dinner at midnight.
examples.chinese: 暑假里，我半夜十二点才吃晚饭。

current_entries.en_definition: A call to do something, especially to come.
(legal) A notice summoning someone to appear in court, as a defendant, juror or witness.
Of or pertaining to the Sundanese people.

(Islam) The largest branch of Islam, followed by around 1.7 billion people, whose adherents believe that Muhammad died without appointing a successor, leading to the election of Abu Bakr as the legitimate leader of the Muslim community.
The time of day when the sun appears above the eastern horizon.

examples.english: What a beautiful sunrise it is!
examples.chinese: 多美的日出啊！

current_entries.en_definition: (figuratively) The final period of the life of a person or thing.

examples.english: We could see the sunset from the window.
examples.chinese: 我们能从窗户看到日落。

current_entries.en_definition: A darkening of the skin resulting from exposure to sunlight or similar light sources.

examples.english: I don't want to get a suntan.
examples.chinese: 我不想晒黑。

current_entries.en_definition: (nutrition, bodybuilding) A vitamin, herbal extract, or chemical compound ingested to meet dietary deficiencies or enhance muscular development; the dosage form that conveys or embodies it (e.g., tablet, capsule, pill, powder, beverage).
(informal) A polite reply to thank you.
A shaped waterproof plank, usually made of wood or foam and reinforced plastic, used to surf on waves.

examples.english: He couldn't wait to try out his new surfboard.
examples.chinese: 他迫不及待地想试一下他的新冲浪板。

current_entries.en_definition: A sudden transient rush, flood or increase.
Specifically, an amount in the public treasury at any time greater than is required for the ordinary purposes of the government.

examples.english: Our surplus has swelled by nearly ten percent in this quarter.
examples.chinese: 我們公司今季的赤字上漲了差不多一成。

current_entries.en_definition: An act of surrendering, submission into the possession of another; abandonment, resignation.

examples.english: I would rather die than surrender.
examples.chinese: 我寧死也不投降。

current_entries.en_definition: An outlying area; area in proximity to something.

examples.english: The surrounding area was very quiet.
examples.chinese: 周圍的地區非常安靜。

current_entries.en_definition: The act of surveying; a general view.
A particular view; an examination, especially an official examination, of a particular group of items, in order to ascertain the condition, quantity, or quality.
An examination of the opinions of a group of people.
(slang) A sailor; a swabby.
(Australia, ambitransitive) To travel on foot carrying a swag (possessions tied in a blanket). [From 1850s.]
(transitive) To bend or shape through use of a swage.

An agglutinative language of the Bantu branch widely spoken in East Africa. Born in its modern form from the hybridization of the Arabic and Bantu cultures, it was the language of the traders in East Africa, and spread along the routes of trade.
A small, migratory bird of the Hirundinidae family with long, pointed, moon-shaped wings and a forked tail which feeds on the wing by catching insects.
Any of various species of large, long-necked waterfowl, of genus Cygnus (bird family: Anatidae), most of which have white plumage.

examples.english: This swan is black.
examples.chinese: 这只天鹅是黑色的。

current_entries.en_definition: An exchange of two comparable things.
A hard stroke, hit or blow, e.g., as part of a spanking.
A bandage; a band.
Influence, weight, or authority that inclines to one side

examples.english: From the time he got up, his beliefs began to sway.
examples.chinese: 从那时起，他的信仰开始摇动了。

current_entries.en_definition: Of or pertaining to Sweden.

examples.english: I speak Swedish.
examples.chinese: 我说瑞典语。

current_entries.en_definition: The language of Sweden and Åland (an autonomous part of Finland). Swedish is also one of the two official languages of Finland, spoken by 6% of the citizens. A very small minority in Estonia has Swedish as their mother tongue.
A long pole, or piece of timber, moved on a horizontal fulcrum fixed to a tall post and used to raise and lower a bucket in a well for drawing water.
Any of the blades of a windmill.
A person who is always very kind.

examples.english: I love you, sweetheart.
examples.chinese: 我愛你，甜心。

current_entries.en_definition: A person very much liked or loved by someone, especially when both partners are young.
A small plain-colored bird of the family Apodidae that resembles a swallow and is noted for its rapid flight.
(by extension) A long draught from a drink. [from 1620s]
(intransitive) To move through the water, without touching the bottom; to propel oneself in water by natural means.

examples.english: You can swim, can't you?
examples.chinese: 你會游泳, 不是嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: The act or art of sustaining and propelling the body in water.

examples.english: Have you ever seen him swimming?
examples.chinese: 你曾看過他游泳嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: (British, colloquial) sophisticated; fashionable; smooth.
(transitive, slang, dated) To flog; to lash.
A faint.
(intransitive, slang, UK, Irish, Commonwealth) To study with effort or determination.

examples.english: I'm a swot.
examples.chinese: 我是个书呆子。

current_entries.en_definition: Of, or relating to synthesis.

examples.english: German is a synthetic language.
examples.chinese: 德语是综合语。

current_entries.en_definition: Artificial, not genuine.
A device consisting of a hypodermic needle, a chamber for containing liquids, and a piston for applying pressure (to inject) or reducing pressure (to draw); a hypodermic syringe.

examples.english: I was calm until I saw the syringe.
examples.chinese: 我很平静，直到我看到了注射器。

current_entries.en_definition: An item of furniture with a flat top surface raised above the ground, usually on one or more legs.

examples.english: Could we have a table outside?
examples.chinese: 我們在外面可以有一張桌子嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: Excluded or forbidden from use, approach or mention.
An inhibition or ban that results from social custom or emotional aversion.
(sports, countable) A play where a player attempts to take control over the ball from an opponent, as in rugby or football.

examples.english: To begin with, we must tackle the problem.
examples.chinese: 首先我們必須處理這個問題。

current_entries.en_definition: A language spoken in the Philippines, especially in areas of central to southern Luzon.
A contamination, decay or putrefaction, especially in food.
Of, from, or pertaining to Tajikistan, the Tajik people (living in Tajikistan and Afghanistan) or the Tajiki dialect of Persian.
The Persian dialect spoken in Tajikistan.
to get hold of something and move it or carry it with you

examples.english: Please take an umbrella with you.
examples.chinese: 請帶一把雨傘。

current_entries.en_definition: to use a form of transport, route, or course of action

examples.english: We took the train to London.
examples.chinese: 我們乘火車前往倫敦。

current_entries.en_definition: to need a particular amount of time or effort

examples.english: The journey takes about two hours.
examples.chinese: 這段旅程大約需要兩小時。

current_entries.en_definition: to accept, choose, or receive something

examples.english: I will take the blue one.
examples.chinese: 我會選藍色的那一個。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To make (an animal) tame; to domesticate.
A plug of cotton or other absorbent material inserted into a body cavity or wound to absorb fluid, especially one inserted in the vagina during menstruation.
Yellowish-brown.

examples.english: He doesn't tan.
examples.chinese: 他晒不黑。

current_entries.en_definition: Having dark skin as a result of exposure to the sun or an artificial process intended to mimic this effect.
A light, brown-like colour.
A darkening of the skin resulting from exposure to sunlight or similar light sources.
The bark of an oak or other tree from which tannic acid is obtained.
A complicated or confused state or condition.
(dance) A standard ballroom dance in 4/4 time; or a social dance, the Argentine tango.
An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun designed for direct fire, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
A follower of Taoism.
A slender wax candle.
A system of government-imposed duties levied on imported or exported goods; a list of such duties, or the duties themselves.
(loosely, UK, Ireland, Canada) Any bituminous road surfacing material.
A difficult or tedious undertaking.
One of the sensations produced by the tongue in response to certain chemicals; the quality of giving this sensation.

An agglutinative language belonging to the Altai group of Turkic languages. It is an official language of Tatarstan. There are some eight million speakers spread across Eastern Europe, Russia and Central Asia.
A vehicle that may be hired for single journeys by members of the public, particularly one with an automated meter to calculate the fare.

examples.english: I went to the airport by taxi.
examples.chinese: 我搭計程車去的機場。

current_entries.en_definition: The act of imposing a tax.
A group of people who favor one side of a binary debate that is divided and lacks a well-established clear consensus.

examples.english: You two are the nucleus of the team.
examples.chinese: 你們倆是團隊的核心。

current_entries.en_definition: (securities and other markets) Relating to the internal mechanics of a market rather than more basic factors.
(intransitive) To tilt back and forth on an edge.
(North America, often, childish) A seesaw; a piece of playground equipment consisting of a long board with seats at either end, with a pivot point in the center.

(chiefly, _, historical) The electrical device gradually developed in the early 19th century to transmit messages (telegrams) using Morse code; the entire system used to transmit its messages including overhead lines and transoceanic cables.

examples.english: Do you know who invented the telegraph?
examples.chinese: 你知道誰發明了電報機嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: (countable, telephony) A telecommunication device (originally mechanical, and now electronic) used for two-way talking with another person (now often shortened to phone).

examples.english: I forget your telephone number.
examples.chinese: 我忘了你的電話號碼。

current_entries.en_definition: A monocular optical instrument that magnifies distant objects, especially in astronomy.
A Dravidian language of India.
(obsolete) To render temperate; to moderate

examples.english: Our country's climate is temperate.
examples.chinese: 我國是溫帶氣候。

current_entries.en_definition: (pottery, architecture) A non-plastic material, such as sand, added to clay to prevent shrinkage and cracking during drying or firing; temper.
Showing signs of stress or strain; not relaxed.

examples.english: He was tense with his new business.
examples.chinese: 他對他的新業務感到緊張。

current_entries.en_definition: (linguistics, grammar, countable) An inflected form of a verb that indicates tense.
A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering people from the weather.

examples.english: It took us half an hour to set up the tent.
examples.chinese: 搭帳篷花了我們半小時。

current_entries.en_definition: The person or thing coming next after the ninth in a series; that which is in the tenth position.

examples.english: School begins on April the tenth.
examples.chinese: 學校在四月十日開學。

current_entries.en_definition: The main language spoken in Thailand (closely related to Lao).

examples.english: How about Thai food?
examples.chinese: 泰国菜怎么样？

current_entries.en_definition: An expression of gratitude or politeness in response to something done or given. [from c. 1400]
Straw, rushes, or similar, used for making or covering the roofs of buildings, or of stacks of hay or grain.
Of or relating to the theatre.

examples.english: Tom and Mary's story is very theatrical.
examples.chinese: 汤姆和玛丽的故事富有戏剧性。

current_entries.en_definition: Of, or relating to therapy.
(location) In or at a place or location (stated, implied or otherwise indicated) that is perceived to be away from, or at a relative distance from, the speaker (compare here).

examples.english: Then there is a problem...
examples.chinese: 那就表示有問題...

current_entries.en_definition: (historical) The eleventh month of the French Republican Calendar, from July 19 or 20 to August 17 or 18.
A plastic with this property.
A device that automatically responds to changes in temperature by activating a heating or cooling system to maintain the temperature at a desired setting.
(historical) Synonym of Nazi Germany.
The cardinal number immediately following thirty and before thirty-two; thirty plus one.
The cardinal number occurring after thirty-six and before thirty-eight.
To defeat utterly.
The cardinal number occurring after two hundred ninety-nine and before three hundred one, represented in Arabic numerals as 300.
(ergative) To suddenly excite someone, or to give someone great pleasure; to (figuratively) electrify; to experience such a sensation.
A group of people crowded or gathered closely together.

examples.english: He was surrounded by a throng of reporters.
examples.chinese: 他被一大群記者包圍著。

current_entries.en_definition: The short thick digit of the hand that for humans has the most mobility and can be made to oppose (moved to touch) all of the other fingers.
(North America) A small nail-like tack with a slightly rounded head that can be pressed into place with light pressure from the thumb; used for hanging light articles on a wall or noticeboard.
(nautical) A seat across a boat on which a rower may sit.
(anatomy) Of, relating to, or being the thyroid gland.
(transitive) To touch repeatedly or stroke delicately in a manner which typically causes laughter, pleasure and twitching.
The daily fluctuation in the level of the sea caused by the gravitational influence of the moon and the sun.
Firmly held together; compact; not loose or open.

examples.english: These shoes are too tight. They hurt.
examples.chinese: 这些鞋子太紧了，伤脚。

current_entries.en_definition: Under high tension; taut.
A person who tills; a farmer.
The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.

examples.english: I don't know if I have the time.
examples.chinese: 我不知道我有沒有時間。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive, communication) To end (an incomplete task) after a time limit.
Done at the proper time or within the proper time limits; prompt.
(mathematics) Multiplied by (see also 'ratio of comparison' sense at 'time).

examples.english: How many times a day do you look at yourself in the mirror?
examples.chinese: 你一天照几次镜子？

current_entries.en_definition: Lacking in courage or confidence.

examples.english: Bob is very timid and blushes when chatting with girls.
examples.chinese: 鮑伯非常膽小，和女生聊天時會臉紅。

current_entries.en_definition: (heraldry) A hue or pattern used in the depiction of a coat of arms.
The degree of vividness of a colour; hue, shade, tint.
A gratuity; a small amount of money left for a bartender, waiter, taxi driver or other servant as a token of appreciation. [from mid-18th c.]

examples.english: Did you leave a tip?
examples.chinese: 你们给了小费吗？

current_entries.en_definition: A piece of private or secret information, especially imparted by someone with expert knowledge about sporting odds, business performance etc. [from mid-19th c.]
(astronomy) : The largest satellite of the planet Uranus.
(idiomatic) Relevant or pertinent; succinct; specific. (The article "the" may be replaced by a possessive form denoting whose point is being acknowledged.)
A sycophant who flatters others to gain personal advantage, or an obsequious, servile lackey or minion.
A proposed salutation (e.g. saying "cheers") while drinking alcohol.
A long sled without runners, with the front end curled upwards, which may be pulled across snow by a cord or used to coast down hills.
Of or pertaining to Tocharian or the Tocharians.
(linguistics) An extinct branch of the Indo-European language family, consisting of two languages, Tocharian A and Tocharian B, written in an abugida derived from Brahmi.
At the same time, in the same place; in close association or proximity.

examples.english: This is my friend Rachel. We went to high school together.
examples.chinese: 这是我朋友Rachel，我们当时在同一所高中。

current_entries.en_definition: Labour, work, especially of a grueling nature.
A fee paid for some liberty or privilege, particularly for the privilege of passing over a bridge or on a highway, or for that of vending goods in a fair, market, etc.

examples.english: We must pay a toll to drive on this road.
examples.chinese: 我们必得缴通行税才能在这条道路上行驶。

current_entries.en_definition: (music) (in a Gregorian chant) A recitational melody.

examples.english: There was a sudden change in her tone.
examples.chinese: 她声调突然变了。

current_entries.en_definition: (language, dated) A whining style of speaking; a kind of mournful or artificial strain of voice; an affected speaking with a measured rhythm and a regular rise and fall of the voice.
A country and archipelago of Polynesia in Oceania. Official name: Kingdom of Tonga. Capital and largest city: Nuku'alofa.
(idiomatic) That's a pity.
Any food item added on top of another, such as sprinkles on ice cream or pepperoni on pizza.
(military) A cylindrical explosive projectile that can travel underwater and is used as a weapon. [from 1805]
A small child.
(of questions, etc.) Difficult or demanding.
(cycle racing) An annual long-distance cycling race through France and other European countries.
A cloth used for wiping, especially one used for drying anything wet, such as a person after a bath.

examples.english: Bring me a dry towel.
examples.chinese: 給我一條乾毛巾。

current_entries.en_definition: Something to play with, especially as intended for use by a child. [from 16th c.]

examples.english: Tom appealed to his mother to buy a new toy for him.
examples.chinese: 汤姆请求妈妈给他买新玩具。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To copy onto a sheet of paper superimposed over the original, by drawing over its lines.

examples.english: He disappeared without a trace.
examples.chinese: 他没有留下痕迹就消失了。

current_entries.en_definition: A particular instance of buying or selling.
A group of animals, vehicles, or people that follow one another in a line, such as a wagon train; a caravan or procession. [from 15th c.]

examples.english: I spent twelve hours on the train.
examples.chinese: 我坐了十二個小時的火車。

current_entries.en_definition: A mechanical (originally steam-powered, now typically diesel or electrical) vehicle carrying a large number of passengers and freight along a designated track or path; a line of connected wagons considered overall as a mode of transport; (as uncountable noun) rail or road travel. [from 19th c.]
(pejorative) A disreputable, promiscuous woman; a slut. [from 1922]
The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.

(LGBTQ) The process or act of changing one's gender role or physical and sexual characteristics, by social, medical, or legal methods, to conform to their identified gender, rather than the sex assigned at birth.

(dated) Of a person, having changed, or being in the process of changing, physical sex by undergoing medical treatment, such as hormone replacement therapy (HRT) and/or optionally sex reassignment surgery (SRS).
(intransitive) To be on a journey, often for pleasure or business and with luggage; to go from one place to another.

examples.english: I don't have anyone who'd travel with me.
examples.chinese: 我没有一个愿意跟我一起旅行的人。

current_entries.en_definition: A foot-operated pedal or lever that generates motion.
A shake, quiver, or vibration.

examples.english: I felt my hands tremble.
examples.chinese: 我感覺到我的手在震動

current_entries.en_definition: A tremble.
A shake, quiver, or vibration.
(geology) A geologic period within the Mesozoic era, comprising lower, middle and upper epochs from about 250 to 200 million years ago.
(hydrology) A natural water stream that flows into a larger river or other body of water.

examples.english: During the 15th century, the Ryukyu Kingdom was a tributary state of Imperial China.
examples.chinese: 在15世紀，琉球王國是中國帝國的附庸國。

current_entries.en_definition: A form of a light-hearted request by which children obtain candy on Halloween while wearing themed costumes.
A very thin river.
A city in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, on the banks of the Moselle river.

examples.english: I'd like a ticket to Trier.
examples.chinese: 我想要張去特里爾的票。

current_entries.en_definition: Slender, lean.

examples.english: Trim the fat off the meat.
examples.chinese: 切除這塊肉的脂肪。

current_entries.en_definition: (uncountable, aviation, of, an aircraft) The state of adjustment of control surfaces such that the desired attitude can be maintained without requiring the continuous application of force to the cockpit controls.

(nautical) The fore-and-aft angle of the vessel to the water, with reference to the cargo and ballast; the manner in which a vessel floats on the water, whether on an even keel or down by the head or stern.
The capital city of Libya, originally three separate settlements known as Oea, Sabratha, and Leptis Magna.
A conclusive success following an effort, conflict, or confrontation of obstacles; victory; conquest.
(geography) Either of the two parallels of latitude 23°26′ north and south of the equator; the farthest points at which the sun can be directly overhead; the boundaries of the torrid zone or tropics.
A mason’s tool, used in spreading and dressing mortar, and breaking bricks to shape them.
A gardener’s tool, shaped like a scoop, used in taking up plants, stirring soil etc.
(historical) An ancient city in what is now northwestern Turkey.
(brass instrument) A musical instrument of the brass family, generally tuned to the key of B-flat; by extension, any type of lip-vibrated aerophone, most often valveless and not chromatic.

examples.english: Ted likes playing the trumpet.
examples.chinese: 泰德喜歡吹小號。

current_entries.en_definition: A melody.

examples.english: The piano was out of tune; it sounded very bad.
examples.chinese: 鋼琴走調了，音色很難聽。

current_entries.en_definition: An underground or underwater passage.

examples.english: On entering a dark tunnel, we could see nothing.
examples.chinese: 走進了漆黑的隧道後，我們甚麼也看不見。

current_entries.en_definition: Of or relating to the Turkic peoples or the Turkic languages.

examples.english: Almaty means "The Father of Apples" in many Turkic languages.
examples.chinese: 在许多突厥语族的语言中，“阿拉木图”有“苹果之父”的意思。

current_entries.en_definition: Any branch of that family.
Of, from, or pertaining to Turkmenistan, the Turkmen people or the Turkmen language.
A Turkic language of the Turkmen spoken mostly in Turkmenistan.
A native or inhabitant of Tuscany, Italy.
A sharp point.
To have a tussle.
One who teaches another (usually called a student, learner, or tutee) in a one-on-one or small-group interaction.
(transitive) To pinch and pull with a sudden jerk and twist; to twitch.
The ordinal form of the number twelve, describing a person or thing in position number 12 of a sequence.

examples.english: The old church on the hill dates back to the twelfth century.
examples.chinese: 山上的老教堂的歷史可以追溯到12世紀。

current_entries.en_definition: The ordinal form of the number twenty.
A sudden bend (or short series of bends) in a road, path, etc.
(transitive) To spot or seek out a bird, especially a rare one.
The cardinal number occurring after one hundred ninety-nine and before two hundred one, represented in Arabic numerals as 200.
Relating to Ukraine or its people or language.

examples.english: When in Ukraine, I always speak Ukrainian.
examples.chinese: 当我在乌克兰时，我总是讲乌克兰语。

current_entries.en_definition: The East Slavic language of Ukrainians, and the official language of Ukraine.
(not comparable) Final; last in a series.

examples.english: What is the ultimate purpose of education?
examples.chinese: 教育的最终目的到底是什么?

current_entries.en_definition: Being the greatest possible; maximum; most extreme.
(not comparable) That will happen at some time; eventual.
Of electromagnetic radiation beyond (higher in frequency than) light visible to the human eye; radiation with wavelengths from 380 to 10 nanometres.

examples.english: Ultraviolet rays can cause skin cancer.
examples.chinese: 紫外線可導致皮膚癌。

current_entries.en_definition: Initialism of United Nations.

examples.english: UN stands for the United Nations.
examples.chinese: UN代表聯合國。

current_entries.en_definition: In secrecy or in hiding.
(transitive) To estimate too low; to perceive (someone or something) as having a lower value, quantity, worth, etc., than what he/she/it actually has.

examples.english: Don't underestimate my power.
examples.chinese: 不要小看我的力量。

current_entries.en_definition: (figuratively) To emphasise or stress something.
A line drawn or printed beneath text; the character _.
To act as an understudy (to someone).
In motion, in progress; being done or carried out; on a journey.
(transitive) To remove the clothing of (someone). [from 17th c.]
(cycling) A pedal cycle that has only one wheel.

examples.english: A unicycle has one wheel.
examples.chinese: 一輛獨輪車有一個輪子。

current_entries.en_definition: A member of a political movement advocating a unitary state rather than a federal one, especially the Unitarios of nineteenth century Argentina (known as the Unitarian Party in English).
(in the singular) An international coalition, founded in 1945 and headquartered in New York City, that aims to promote global co-operation and maintain international order.
(in the plural) The collection of individual states of the United States of America.
Common to all members of a group or class.

examples.english: English is a universal language and is used all over the world.
examples.chinese: 英語是一種世界性的語言，用於世界各地。

current_entries.en_definition: Unlimited; vast; infinite.
(transitive) To bring (a person) up to date: to inform (a person) about recent developments.

examples.english: Could I get you to update this data for me?
examples.chinese: 你可以幫我更新這份資料嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive, transitive, computing) To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Located on a higher floor or level of a building.

examples.english: Would you carry my luggage upstairs?
examples.chinese: 你可以把我的行李拿到樓上嗎?

current_entries.en_definition: Up the stairs; on or to a higher floor or level.
Modern Standard Urdu, an Indo-Aryan language with native speakers mainly in Pakistan and North India. It is a standardized and Persianized version of Hindustani.
(transitive) To press; to push; to drive; to impel; to force onward.

examples.english: Advertisements urge us to buy luxuries.
examples.chinese: 廣告慫恿我們買奢侈品。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To be pressing in argument; to insist; to persist.
(transitive) To employ; to apply; to utilize.

examples.english: So annoying... Now I get a headache whenever I use the computer!
examples.chinese: 好煩……現在我只要一用電腦就會頭痛！

current_entries.en_definition: (temporal location) Formerly and habitually or repeatedly, but possibly no longer, did.
A person, in a church, cinema etc., who escorts people to their seats.
A male escort at a wedding.
(business) A commodity or service provided on a continuous basis by a physical infrastructure network, such as electricity, water supply or sewerage.
(business, finance, by extension) A natural or legal monopoly distributer of such a utility; or, the securities of such a provider.
The most extreme; greatest, ultimate.

examples.english: This is a matter of the utmost importance.
examples.chinese: 这件事至关重要。

current_entries.en_definition: The language of Uzbekistan.
(dated) A person who wanders from place to place; a nomad, a wanderer.
(specifically) A person without settled employment or habitation who usually supports himself or herself by begging or some dishonest means; a tramp, a vagabond.
(professional wrestling) A female performer in professional wrestling, acting as either a manager or personal chaperone; often used to attract and titillate male members of the audience.
(US) A person employed to clean or park cars.
The top part of a boot or shoe, above the sole and welt and in front of the ankle seam, that covers the instep and toes; the front part of an upper; the analogous part of a stocking. [from c. 1225]
(Hinduism) Any of a large body of texts originating in Ancient India. They form the oldest layer of Sanskrit literature and the oldest sacred texts of Hinduism.
Relating to vegans or veganism. [from 1944]

examples.english: I am vegan.
examples.chinese: 我是纯素食者。

current_entries.en_definition: A person who does not eat animal flesh, or, in some cases, use any animal products. [from 1839]

examples.english: Can you recommend any vegetarian dishes?
examples.chinese: 可以介紹幾道素菜嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: (anatomy) A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.
A closely woven fabric (originally of silk, now also of cotton or man-made fibres) with a thick short pile on one side.
An opening in a volcano from which lava or gas flows.
Word for word; in exactly the same words as were used originally.
A blue-green patina or rust that forms on copper-containing metals.
Knowledgeable or skilled, either through study or experience; familiar; practiced.
Standing, pointing, or moving straight up or down; parallel to the local direction of gravity; along the direction of a plumb line; perpendicular to something horizontal.
To a great extent or degree.

examples.english: My mom doesn't speak English very well.
examples.chinese: 我妈妈英语说得不太好。

current_entries.en_definition: (North America) A sleeveless garment that buttons down the front, worn over a shirt, and often as part of a suit; a waistcoat.

examples.english: You need to reverse your vest, it's back to front.
examples.chinese: 背心反了， 要翻一個面。

current_entries.en_definition: A sleeveless top, typically with identifying colours or logos, worn by an athlete or member of a sports team.
To thoroughly check or investigate particularly with regard to providing formal approval.
Of or relating to the medical or surgical treatment of non-human animals, especially domestic and farm animals.

examples.english: Tom has a veterinary background.
examples.chinese: Tom有獸醫背景。

current_entries.en_definition: (informal) Vibration. [later 20th c.]

examples.english: This place gives me a really bad vibe.
examples.chinese: 这地方给我一个很差的印象。

current_entries.en_definition: Of or relating to the reign of Queen Victoria from 1837 to 1901, or that period.
(archaic) Food fit for human (or occasionally animal) consumption.
That which is vile; vileness.
Of a violet colour.
Specifically, a musician (or other performer) with masterly ability, technique, or personal style. [from 18th c.]
A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.

examples.english: I applied for a visa.
examples.chinese: 我申请了签证。

current_entries.en_definition: (phonetics) Consisting of, or characterized by, voice, or tone produced in the larynx, which may be modified, either by resonance, as in the case of the vowels, or by obstructive action, as in certain consonants, such as v, l, etc., or by both, as in the nasals m, n, ng.

examples.english: Public feeling against air pollution has at last become vocal.
examples.chinese: 公众终于对关于空气污染的意见直言不讳了。

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar) Used in address; appellative; said of the case or form of the noun, pronoun, or adjective by which a person or thing is addressed.
(of a person) Quick to become angry or violent.
(of a situation) Potentially violent.
Done by design or intention; intentional.
Working or done without payment.
Any of a group of related religious practices found chiefly in and around the Caribbean, particularly in Haiti and Louisiana.
action of the verb to vote
To crumple or crush into a compact, amorphous shape or ball.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern, often eaten hot with butter and/or honey or syrup.

examples.english: Grease the waffle maker.
examples.chinese: 給鬆餅機塗上油。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To be moved, or to pass, on a buoyant medium; to float.
(intransitive, mostly, obsolete) To go; to proceed; to move; to progress.
(intransitive) To cry out, as in sorrow or anguish.
(intransitive) To make a noise like mourning or crying.
(transitive) To lament; to bewail; to grieve over.
A female attendant who serves customers in a restaurant, café, or similar.

examples.english: Waitress, I'd like to order.
examples.chinese: 服务生，我要点菜。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) (often followed by up) To make somebody stop sleeping; to rouse from sleep.

examples.english: You had better not wake me up when you come in.
examples.chinese: 你进来的时候最好不要把我弄醒。

examples.english: I live within walking distance of school.
examples.chinese: 我學校離我家只是幾步路的路程。

current_entries.en_definition: Of, from or relating to Wallonia.
A Romance language traditionally spoken in parts of southern Belgium and a small strip of northern France (around Givet).
(transitive) To cover (a wall, a room, etc) with wallpaper.
Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually but not always involving active engagement of military forces.

examples.english: A nuclear war will bring about the destruction of mankind.
examples.chinese: 核戰爭將會為人類帶來滅亡。

current_entries.en_definition: A part of a hospital, with beds, where patients reside.
(Mormonism) A subdivision of the LDS Church, smaller than and part of a stake, but larger than a branch.
A person under guardianship.
A place for storing large amounts of products. In logistics, a place where products go to from the manufacturer before going to the retailer.

examples.english: Who was it that forgot to lock the door of the warehouse?
examples.chinese: 是誰忘了把倉庫的門鎖上？

current_entries.en_definition: Used to warn of danger in signs and notices.
The action of the verb warn; an instance of warning someone.
(weaving) The threads that run lengthwise in a woven fabric; crossed by the woof or weft.
(law, countable) A judicial writ authorizing an officer to make a search, seizure, or arrest, or to execute a judgment.
A broad ski used for water skiing.
A regional municipality and city in Ontario, Canada.

examples.english: The French were defeated at Waterloo.
examples.chinese: 法國人在滑鐵盧之役吃了敗仗。

current_entries.en_definition: (archaic) To sway back and forth, as if about to fall; to reel, to stagger, to totter.
Situated near the side of a road or path.
Any plant unwanted at the place where and at the time when it is growing.

examples.english: He pulled up the weed and threw it away.
examples.chinese: 他拔起杂草就扔掉。

current_entries.en_definition: (uncountable, informal, originally, slang) Cannabis.
The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in Western countries, Saturday and Sunday.

examples.english: Did you have a good weekend?
examples.chinese: 你週末過得怎麼樣？

current_entries.en_definition: (weightlifting) An object, such as a weight plate or barbell, used for strength training.

examples.english: What's the weight of your suitcase?
examples.chinese: 你的行李多重？

current_entries.en_definition: (history) The democratic regime of Germany from 1919 to the assumption of power by Adolf Hitler in 1933.
(transitive) To join two materials (especially two metals) together by applying heat, pressure and filler, either separately or in any combination.
A member of a Slavic people from the borders of Germany and Poland; a Sorb; a Kashub.

The hemisphere in Earth to the west of the Greenwich meridian (zero degree longitude) and east of 180 degrees longitude (approximately the International Date Line), containing the Americas and a small part of eastern Russia.
Any one of numerous large marine mammals comprising an informal group within infraorder Cetacea that usually excludes dolphins and porpoises.

examples.english: Throw a sprat to catch a whale.
examples.chinese: 丟鲱鱼抓鯨魚。

current_entries.en_definition: (nautical) An artificial landing place for ships on a riverbank or shore.
(colloquial) For what reason; why.
Regardless of the ... that; for any ... that.

examples.english: Whatever I do, she says I can do better.
examples.chinese: 不管我做什么，她都说我能做得更好。

current_entries.en_definition: A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.
(archaic) Because of which.

examples.english: O Romeo Romeo, wherefore art thou Romeo?
examples.chinese: 罗密欧啊罗密欧，你为什么要是罗密欧呢？
